revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:造成温泉]]
*{{ill|Constructed Hot Spring|ja|造成温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constructed Hot Spring|qq=}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:22, 25 March 2025 (UTC)|qq=}} [[File:Oowakudani_200507.JPG|thumb|'''Zōsei Onsen''' in Owakudani]] '''Constructed Hot Spring''' ('''Zōsei Onsen''') is an artificially created hot spring produced by methods such as channeling water through the ground or steam, and it meets the standards of {{ill|Hot Spring Law|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Law|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}. It is primarily a form of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} observed in volcanic regions where high-temperature volcanic gases are emitted from {{ill|Volcano|en|Volcano|qid=Q8072|lt=Volcano|1=Volcano}} areas. == Overview == * Even without drilling deep {{ill|Boring (earth)|en|Boring (earth)|qid=Q12177444|lt=Borehole drilling|1=Boring (earth)}} that involves high risks and costs, there is the advantage of obtaining high-quality {{ill|Hot Spring Source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} by installing a {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563768}} or a simple apparatus. * Although in the past artificial hot springs had a strong image of being man-made and were poorly rated, they are being re-evaluated in recent years because they can secure the large volumes of water required for the increasingly popular {{ill|Flow-Through Onsen|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flow-Through Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}. In fact, whether the water is naturally obtained as groundwater or acquired artificially, the resulting hot spring water possesses an exceptionally high quality. * When utilizing mountain water, one can obtain hot spring water of excellent quality; however, in locations where large quantities of mountain water are not available, {{ill|Water supply|en|Water supply|qid=Q1061108|lt=Municipal Water Supply|1=Water supply}} may be used, in which case the {{ill|Chlorine|en|Chlorine|qid=Q688|lt=Chlorine|1=Chlorine}} contained in the water can affect its quality. * In hot spring areas (volcanic regions) where the source is obtained by shallow drilling, there also exist so-called semi-constructed hot springs in which the water volume is significantly increased by injecting water from the surface. * In areas such as {{ill|Hakone|ja|箱根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakone|1=Hakone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11603342}}’s {{ill|Hakone Onsen|en|Hakone Onsen|qid=Q908993|lt=Owakudani Hot Springs|1=Hakone Onsen}}, companies like Hakone Onsen Supply Co., Ltd. have long produced constructed hot springs and supplied them to local hot spring inns. == External Links == * [http://www.hakoneonsen.com Hakone Onsen Supply Co., Ltd.] {{Onsen|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|造成温泉|version=69777857|comment=Imported full ja history}}
